summary: Wrote ledger-v1 for gto-poker-async-duel. Telos: GitHub-Pages-hostable static web app for two-player async head-to-head GTO poker quiz with per-decision confidence ratings, opt-in turn notifications, and end-of-game disagreement wrap-up. Enumerated 2 proper nouns (PN.1 'GTO Poker Training' demoted to supportive — names ancestor build runs/gto-poker-trainer/, not target; PN.2 'GitHub Pages' as target_defining requiring Researcher canonical verification of current static-hosting constraints). 20 assumptions logged; 8 inflection points (2 high-importance: IP1 async state transport on static host, IP2 cross-user notification delivery — both flagged researcher_dispatched and tightly coupled). 8 first-contact requirements derived for Tier 2 verification. 14 explicit out-of-scope items. Concerns surfaced for TD (IP1+IP2 coupling, GitHub Pages subpath SW scope gotchas, security-rules-not-secrecy for BaaS keys) and Editor (emotional context load-bearing, new artifact needs a distinguishing name).

Dispatched Probe 001 to investigate IP1 (async state transport on GitHub Pages) and IP2 (cross-user notification delivery) as a coupled pair per Discovery's concern note. Budget 30 min; max 4 options; optimization criterion fit_to_project_goals.

Evaluated 4 options: Firebase Spark, Supabase free, no-backend URL/code, GitHub Gist. Recommended Firebase Spark with W3C VAPID Web Push (not FCM HTTP v1 — incompatible with client-only static-site architecture; the VAPID-protocol Web Push works against Chrome/Edge/Firefox/iOS via their respective endpoints). Disqualifying findings: Supabase free-tier auto-pauses inactive projects after 7 days, which fatally contradicts the prompt's weekly-cadence async use case; no-backend URL approach has no server actor to fire push when recipient tab is closed; Gist anonymous-write is deprecated and has no native push partner. 6 verbatim citations captured from canonical sources (GitHub Docs, Firebase Pricing, Apple Developer Docs, WebKit blog, Supabase docs).

Wrote sections-v1.json with 7 sections (6 product + 1 edge-case-testing), 8 inflection_resolutions (IP1 and IP2 sourced from canonical_evidence with citations_pointer; IP3-IP8 via quick reasoning, source: discovery or td_plan). 54 discovery_coverage_assertions covering every load-bearing prose field in Discovery's ledger (restatement, telos, 20 assumptions, 8 inflection-point topics, 2 proper nouns, 8 first-contact requirements, 14 out-of-scope items). 1 prompt_named_verb_assertion (PNV.1) exercising a full 2-round / 2-profile play-through under production fidelity. prompt_verb_analysis chose 'play' as the load-bearing verb. Per-section acceptance_assertions across all 7 sections (40+ assertions). Source-field discipline: external-system claims (GitHub Pages, Apple iOS push, Firebase quotas, Web Push protocol) use source: canonical_evidence with citations_pointer; internal-consistency claims use source: td_plan; prompt-derived coverage uses source: prompt.

Wrote 11 interface contracts covering every section dependency edge: section-1->section-2 (types + scenario API), section-1->section-4 (types + getScenarioById), section-1->section-6 (boot listScenarios), section-2->section-3 (push-subscription persistence), section-2->section-4 (readGame + submitHandful with idempotency), section-2->section-5 (createGame + joinGame with error-tagging), section-2->section-6 (initFirebase + signInAnonymously), section-3->section-4 (enableNotifications + sendTurnNotification + notificationStatus), section-3->section-6 (registerServiceWorker), section-4->section-6 (mountInGameView + mountWrapUpView), section-5->section-6 (landing/create/join/waiting mount APIs).

Verdict: pass_with_recommendations. Six structural checks executed against TD's sections-v1 and Discovery's ledger-v1. Checks 2 (high-importance Discovery action), 3 (td_ip_source), 4 (first_contact_coverage), 5 (telos_coherence), and 6 (prompt_named_verb_quality) all pass. Check 1 (proper_noun_citation) surfaced two low/medium structural lifecycle gaps in Discovery's record-keeping (PN.2 status never transitioned from 'pending' to 'verified' despite Researcher dispatch + CIT.1 verbatim; PN.1 demotion_note exists inline but no demotion-v1.json artifact emitted) — TD's substantive compliance is intact, both flagged as Discovery record-keeping cleanup, non-blocking. The 'fully hostable on GitHub Pages' constraint is judged honestly interpreted: the deliverable IS a static directory hosted by Pages; Firebase is a backend service the app calls (not a self-hosted server). Four-step user setup (create Firebase project, paste config, push, enable Pages) is acknowledged via S6.A4 README and S5.A5 onboarding copy; flagged as a recommendation that Verifier treat README clarity as load-bearing. Four findings (1 medium F.1, 2 low F.2 F.4, 1 medium F.3); zero routes (no route_to_discovery, no route_to_td). Four recommendations issued covering: README deliverability emphasis, Discovery record-keeping cleanup, experiential quality of wrap-up + notes, and a name pick for the new artifact (recommend 'GTO Duel').
Built DAG with 7 waves: w1 section-1, w2 section-2, w3 section-3+section-5 parallel, w4 section-4, w5 section-6, w6 integrator pseudo-node, w7 section-7. Dispatch mode: inline.

State/Backend Adapter (state.js) + Firestore Security Rules. Firebase JS SDK 10.12.5 from gstatic CDN. Anonymous Auth, Firestore game-document model with participantUids + participants[] + rounds[] + status. Idempotent submitHandful via runTransaction overwrite. UI-layer redactGameForViewer for defense-in-depth on answer blindness. Rules deny non-participant read/write and cap participants at 2.

Logged client-side VAPID signing decision (implementation_path_chosen) — implements Web Push by signing JWT client-side using configured VAPID private key and POSTing directly to the recipient's pushSubscription.endpoint. Avoids requiring user to deploy Cloud Functions; security tradeoff documented in README.

Notifications + Service Worker. push.js implements W3C VAPID Web Push (client-signed JWT, ES256 P-256). sw.js handles push + notificationclick. manifest.json display:standalone. iOS Add-to-Home-Screen detection + onboarding copy.

Pairing + Onboarding. mountLandingView/mountCreateGameView/mountJoinGameView/mountWaitingForOpponentView. ?join=<code> URL parameter is auto-detected and pre-fills the join code. Inline iOS Add-to-Home-Screen copy when ios_requires_home_screen_install is true.

Game Flow + UI. flow.js computePhase state machine. stats.js perPlayerAccuracy/interPlayerAgreement/rankedDisagreements (joint-confidence-min descending). ui.js mountInGameView (handful submission, waiting, reveal) + mountWrapUpView (3 sections: per-player accuracy, agreement, ranked disagreements). Per-scenario 1-5 confidence + optional 280-char note. Notification-enable button in game header, click-gated.

Controller + Shell. index.html, src/app.js boot order (loadScenarios -> initFirebase -> signInAnonymously -> registerServiceWorker -> mountRouter). config.js with FIREBASE_CONFIG + VAPID placeholders. README.md with the 10-step user setup (covers the four canonical steps plus auxiliary steps). All asset references relative; SW scope = './'.

Assembled deliverable: index.html, manifest.json (Web App Manifest display:standalone), sw.js, firestore.rules, README.md, _build-manifest.json (integrator's own), src/{app,state,scenarios,push,onboarding,ui,flow,stats,config}.js, data/scenarios.json, styles/app.css, icons/icon-{192,512}.svg. Static cross-check: zero absolute-root asset URLs; firebase imports only in src/state.js; zero gto-poker-trainer references; local static server returns HTTP 200 for every asset under a /my-repo/ subpath.

Logged contract_micro_adjustment: integrator build-manifest moved to _build-manifest.json (instead of manifest.json) because manifest.json at project root is the load-bearing W3C Web App Manifest required for iOS PWA install.

Edge-case testing. 32/32 assertions PASS in Node-runnable test suite: scenario shape, sampleNScenarios determinism, charter API surfaces, Firestore rules predicate, no-firebase-leak, idempotent overwrite, SW registration, manifest display, click-gated permission, redaction, joint-confidence-min ranking (engineered (5,5)/(5,2)/(3,3) game), per-player accuracy + inter-player agreement math, state-flow transitions, 2-participant cap, no native build artifacts, no non-anonymous auth, no abs-root assets, four-step README, share-URL routing, etc. PNV.1 mechanical clauses all PASS; live-browser dynamic clauses deferred to first deploy.
All sections verified; integrator verified; edge-case testing executed (32/32). Awaiting Critic final-sweep and Convergence Verifier.
